channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Dozens in Border Patrol custody after boat runs out of fuel near San Diego
item.description: A Coast Guard crew deployed a boarding team and was able to safely take 26 people representing multiple nationalities to shore
item.dc:creator: Anna Ashcraft, NewsNation

item.title: A federal judge temporarily blocks Trump’s executive order ending birthright citizenship
item.description: SEATTLE (AP) — A federal judge on Thursday temporarily blocked President Donald Trump’s executive order redefining birthright citizenship, calling it “blatantly unconstitutional” during the first hearing in a multi-state effort challenging the order. U.S.
item.dc:creator: EUGENE JOHNSON and MIKE CATALINI, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: What we know so far about the Antioch High School shooter
item.description: Solomon Henderson arrived at the school on a bus and is believed to have gone into the restroom and retrieved a pistol.
item.dc:creator: Alicia Patton, NewsNation

item.title: Experts worry that Trump's Jan. 6 pardons will legitimize political violence, embolden extremists
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — After President Donald Trump pardoned around 1,500 Jan. 6 Capitol rioters on Monday, far-right activists cheered the move and said it strengthened their loyalty to him. Some also borrowed from the president’s own rhetoric, calling for retribution.
item.dc:creator: ALI SWENSON and LINDSAY WHITEHURST, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Disneyland may remove longtime Haunted Mansion element, Imagineer says
item.description: The creative team at Disneyland has considered removing one of the Haunted Mansion’s many “frightful delights,” according to an Imagineer.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: LIVE: Ship stuck in ice off Lake Erie shoreline in Buffalo
item.description: BUFFALO, N.Y. (WIVB) — A lake freighter ship is stuck in ice in the Lake Erie shoreline in the Buffalo River. The vessel Manitoulin, which was returning to Michigan after completing a routine delivery of rock salt, became immobilized by thick ice, which the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Aidan Joly, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump won't ban immigration arrests at churches. Now clergy are weighing how to resist
item.description: Numerous faith leaders across the U.S. say the immigration crackdown launched by President Donald Trump’s new administration has sown fear within their migrant-friendly congregations.
item.dc:creator: Giovanna Dell'Orto, Luis Andres Henao And Deepa Bharath, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Senate GOP full steam ahead on Hegseth despite abuse allegations
item.description: Senate Republicans are steamrolling ahead on Pete Hegseth’s nomination to lead the Pentagon, and a new report detailing allegations of abusive behavior by the nominee have seemingly not dissuaded them.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, NewsNation

item.title: CNN is announcing layoffs as part of a further shift to digital business
item.description: CNN expects to lay off about 200 people as part of a restructuring announced on Thursday that pivots the news organization toward a more digitally oriented future.
item.dc:creator: DAVID BAUDER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: With Trump pivot back to pro-oil and gas policies, one renewable energy finds favor
item.description: As promised, President Donald Trump began reversing the country's energy policies his first day in office with a spate of orders largely favoring oil, gas and coal. But there is one renewable energy that did find favor: geothermal.
item.dc:creator: JENNIFER McDERMOTT, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump's new Justice Department leadership orders a freeze on civil rights cases
item.description: Attorneys in the department's Civil Rights Division were ordered not to file any new complaints, amicus briefs or other certain court papers “until further notice," one of the memos said.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Costco stands by its DEI policies as other companies scale back
item.description: Costco is pushing back on a shareholder proposal that urges the wholesale club operator to conduct an evaluation of any business risks posed by its diversity, equity and inclusion practices. Investors were expected to vote on the recommendation during the company's annual meeting Thursday.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump says he may withhold federal aid for Los Angeles fires
item.description: The president repeated false claims that the state's fish conservation efforts in the northern part of the state are responsible for fire hydrants running dry in urban areas.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Las Vegas hotel workers union reaches deal with casino to end longest strike in decades
item.description: LAS VEGAS (AP) — The longest strike in decades by Las Vegas hotel workers ended Wednesday with a new contract between the union and a casino where hundreds of employees had walked off the job in November.
item.dc:creator: RIO YAMAT, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Idaho murders suspect Bryan Kohberger to appear in court as defense challenges prosecution methods
item.description: Idaho murders suspect Bryan Kohberger will return to court for the first time in more than two months to argue for excluding nearly all evidence collected by police.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Aerial attack helps firefighters maintain the upper hand on a huge fire north of Los Angeles
item.description: Firefighters are battling to maintain the upper hand on a huge and rapidly moving wildfire that swept through rugged mountains north of Los Angeles.
item.dc:creator: Christopher Weber And Marcio Jose Sanchez, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Oath Keepers founder Stewart Rhodes visits Capitol Hill after Trump clemency
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Oath Keepers founder Stewart Rhodes, who was convicted of orchestrating his far-right extremist group’s Jan. 6, 2021 assault on the U.S.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL KUNZELMAN and LISA MASCARO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Nancy Leftenant-Colon, who was the first Black woman in Army Nurse Corps, has died
item.description: The first Black woman to join the U.S. Army Nurse Corps after the military was desegregated in the 1940s has died. She was 104.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL CASEY, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Indiana couple abused 33 kids over 13 years: Docs
item.description: Details have been released on how 33 children were treated in the Indiana home of reported abusers Sonja and Brian Stafford for approximately 13 years.
item.dc:creator: Deja Studdard, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Former Arkansas officer arrested, charged with assault in beating of handcuffed inmate
item.description: LITTLE ROCK, Ark. (AP) — A former Arkansas police officer who was caught on video beating a handcuffed inmate in the back of his patrol car last year has been arrested and charged with aggravated assault.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Disaster prep: How to know if you're underinsured
item.description: Those who are hoping to rebuild could be in for an unwelcome surprise. Here's what to know about underinsurance.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Dorn, NewsNation

item.title: Budweiser Clydesdales to make Tampa Bay appearance
item.description: Known for their majestic stature and storied history, the Budweiser mascots are a fan favorite wherever they go.
item.dc:creator: Brody Wooddell, NewsNation

item.title: Trump pumps brakes on Day 1 tariffs
item.description: President Donald Trump pumped the brakes on his plan to deliver wide-ranging tariffs as soon as he took office, slow-tracking and toning down changes to the U.S. trade system that figured as a centerpiece of his campaign.
item.dc:creator: Tobias Burns, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Teen fatally shoots 16-year-old girl, himself at Tennessee high school: police
item.description: A student is dead and another injured after a teen gunman opened fire at a Nashville area high school before turning the gun on himself, police say.
item.dc:creator: Brittney Baird, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Starbucks' policy change flushes out a debate over public restroom access
item.description: Starbucks’ decision to restrict its restrooms to paying customers has flushed out a wider problem: a patchwork of restroom policies that has left Americans confused and divided over who gets to use the loo and when. Rules about restroom access in restaurants vary by state, city and county.
item.dc:creator: DEE-ANN DURBIN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Teen fatally shoots 16-year-old girl, himself at Tennessee high school: police
item.description: A student is dead and another injured after a teen gunman opened fire at a Nashville area high school before turning the gun on himself, police say.
item.dc:creator: Brittney Baird, KRON4

item.title: Mexico setting up tents for migrants deported by Trump
item.description: Mexico on Tuesday continued to prepare for President Donald Trump’s promised mass deportations by setting up the first of nine migrant attention centers on the border.
item.dc:creator: Julian Resendiz, Fox 5

item.title: GOP congressman wants bishop 'added to deportation list' after Trump prayer service
item.description: Rep. Mike Collins heavily criticized statements made by the Right Rev. Mariann Budde on Tuesday at the inaugural prayer service held for President Trump.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, Fox 5

item.title: 2 students dead after shooting at Antioch High School; 17-year-old shooter identified
item.description: Two students are dead and two are injured following a shooting at Antioch High School Wednesday morning.
item.dc:creator: Brittney Baird, NewsNation

item.title: Justice Department directs prosecutors to investigate local efforts to obstruct immigration enforcement
item.description: The Justice Department is directing its federal prosecutors to investigate for potential criminal charges any state or local officials who stand in the way of beefed-up enforcement of immigration laws under the Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Trump popularity higher than throughout most of first term: Poll
item.description: The poll found Trump with a 47 percent approval rating, higher than almost the entirety of his first term, though still below the majority support that most other presidents have enjoyed at the start of their administrations.
item.dc:creator: Jared Gans, KRON4

item.title: Trump targets Alaska's oil and other resources as environmentalists gear up for a fight
item.description: JUNEAU, Alaska (AP) — President Donald Trump's expansive executive order aimed at boosting oil and gas drilling, mining and logging in Alaska is being cheered by state political leaders who see new fossil fuel development as critical to Alaska's economic future and criticized by environmental groups
item.dc:creator: BECKY BOHRER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: As fire-fanning winds continue, Southern California readies for potential rain and toxic runoff
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Parched Southern California continued to face dangerous winds but could get some badly needed rain this weekend, forecasters said Tuesday, dampening the prospects of another round of killer wildfires though even a small amount of precipitation could could create new challenges lik
item.dc:creator: CHRISTOPHER WEBER, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Trump administration throws out policies limiting migrant arrests at sensitive spots like churches
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Officers enforcing immigration laws will now be able to arrest migrants at sensitive locations like schools and churches after the Trump administration threw out policies limiting where those arrests could happen as the new president seeks to make good on campaign promises to carry
item.dc:creator: REBECCA SANTANA, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Trump rolls back Biden directive to study methods of lowering prescription costs
item.description: Among the dozens of Biden-era executive orders he rescinded on day one, President Trump nixed one that aimed to explore new health care payment models that would lower prescription costs for Medicare and Medicaid beneficiaries.
item.dc:creator: Joseph Choi, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Inauguration prayer service's pastor asks Trump to 'have mercy' on transgender kids, immigrants
item.description: “I ask you to have mercy upon the people in our country who are scared. There are gay, lesbian, transgender children, Democratic, Republican, independent families — some who fear for their lives,” she said.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, Fox 5

item.title: America's worst traffic isn't in Los Angeles. These cities are worse
item.description: Los Angeles might have a bad rap when it comes to commute times, but new data from GPS technology experts TomTom found it's not even the worst city in the U.S. when it comes to bumper-to-bumper traffic.
item.dc:creator: Travis Schlepp, KTLA 5
